rules.title: PDSPrj is ZIP-style container
rules.rule: .pdsprj files begin with PK and contain internal project files such as PROJECT.XML, ROOT.DSN, ROOT.CDB, and SCRIPTS/PWRRAILS.DAT.

rules.title: ROOT.DSN controls visual object existence
rules.rule: Components visible in ROOT.DSN appear in Proteus even if ROOT.CDB is missing entries; CDB-only components do not create visible schematic objects.

rules.title: ROOT.CDB controls resistor values and references
rules.rule: For existing resistors, ROOT.CDB is authoritative for value and reference designator. ROOT.DSN display metadata can be normalized from ROOT.CDB on open/save.

rules.title: Terminal labels live in ROOT.DSN
rules.rule: INPUT, OUTPUT, POWER, and GROUND terminal objects and labels are stored in ROOT.DSN. Terminal-only changes do not alter ROOT.CDB.

rules.title: Topology is ROOT.DSN-controlled
rules.rule: Series/parallel/ladder topology changes are represented in ROOT.DSN through terminal labels, wire stubs, and object layout. ROOT.CDB can remain identical across different topologies with the same component refs/values.

rules.title: ROOT.CDB must exist
rules.rule: Removing ROOT.CDB entirely causes fatal Proteus/ISIS failure. A generator must include ROOT.CDB, even if minimal.

rules.title: PWRRAILS.DAT unchanged in early terminal/passive tests
rules.rule: Visible VCC/GND terminals and resistor networks did not modify SCRIPTS/PWRRAILS.DAT. For early generator versions, copy this file unchanged from a template.

rules.title: V9 resistor generator uses linked endpoint groups
rules.rule: For generated resistor networks, emit matching input/output terminal records, a resistor visual record, and two short-wire records with consistent link suffixes and only one final terminator at the end of the object stream.

rules.title: V9 resistor generation uses E001 as base
rules.rule: Use the blank E001 project as the .pdsprj container base for resistor generation. Donor projects may provide byte-record templates, but generated outputs replace ROOT.CDB and ROOT.DSN and copy PROJECT.XML/PWRRAILS from E001.

rules.title: Power and ground endpoint terminals are label-width constrained
rules.rule: For the locked resistor V9 method, use two-character labels such as V0 and G0. $TERPOWER is safe as a left endpoint replacement for $TERINPUT; $TERGROUND is safe as a right endpoint replacement for $TEROUTPUT.
